titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of TNA DL 25/84

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=846 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.126145+00 | translation moa=847 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.11438+00

p.cl: Omnibus hoc scriptum visuris uel audituris Ricardus de Bancori salutem

Sciatis me quietam cla
masse pro me et heredibus meis Imperpetuum domino meo Roberto de Brus . et heredibus suis totam terram
de loyerwode per has diuisas . videlicet de Polhulin vsque Blakebecke . sicut medietas mos
se extendit . et de dicta medietate mosse  vsque aquam de loyer cum Pertinenciis . cum quadam communi
pastura In feodo de Comlongan quam homines dicti Roberti de Brus de misefaud de me
solebant habere ad firmam . pro vna marca annuatim soluenda . saluo cum mihi et heredibus meis in
bosco meo ... gigo . sicut ante diem huius quiete clamacionis ipsum b...uus de
nui .
In cuius rei testimonium huic scripto sigillum meum apposui .

Hiis testibus . Dominis .
Ada de Carnoto . Dauid de Parco . Vmfrido de Kirkepatric . Rogero de Kirkepatric . Willelmo de
sancto mich’ . Engramo de musseus . Iacobo Clerico . et Aliis .
To all who shall view or hear this writing Richard of Banchory sends greeting.

Know me to have quitclaimed for myself and for my heirs for ever to my lord, Robert de Brus, and his heirs, the entire land of Locharwoods by these boundaries, namely, from 'Polhulin' to 'Blakebecke', just as it extends halfway along the mosse, and from the said middle of the mosse to the Water of Lochar; with pertinents, with a common pasture in the feu of Comlongon which the men of the said Robert de Brus of Mouswald used to have at ferme from me, in return for one merk to be paid yearly, reserving to myself and my heirs in my wood of ..., just as before the day of this quitclaim
In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to this writing.
